name: Hostfully to Operto Reservation Cleaning Sync

nodes.name: Fetch Hostfully Leads1
nodes.notes:
Fetches the next page of reservations using the cursor.
Why: some properties have more bookings than fit in one API page.

nodes.name: Update Reservation with Cleaning Job ID
nodes.notes:
Links the reservation to its cleaning job by writing the job reference back.
Why: keeps Reservations and CleaningJobs cross-referenced.

nodes.name: Create Cleaning Job Record
nodes.notes:
Appends a new row to the CleaningJobs tab.
Why: creates the job that Phase 2 will pick up and assign to a cleaner.

nodes.name: Prepare Cleaning Job Data
nodes.notes:
Builds the cleaning job fields (date, time, status=PENDING, empty cleanerId).
Why: structures the data before writing to CleaningJobs.

nodes.name: Create Reservation Record
nodes.notes:
Appends a new row to the Reservations tab with booking details.
Why: this is our internal record of the booking.

nodes.name: Cleaning Job Needed?
nodes.notes:
Checks if a CleaningJobs row already exists for this booking.
Why: prevents duplicate cleaning job records.

nodes.name: Reservation Exists?
nodes.notes:
Branches: TRUE = already in sheet (skip), FALSE = new booking (create).
Why: prevents duplicate reservation records.

nodes.name: Merge Lead and Lookup
nodes.notes:
Combines the Hostfully data with any existing reservation data.
Why: gives us all fields in one object for the next decisions.

nodes.name: Ensure One Item
nodes.notes:
Guarantees exactly one item passes through, even if lookup returned nothing.
Why: downstream nodes expect one item per iteration.

nodes.name: Lookup Reservation
nodes.notes:
Checks if this bookingUid already exists in the Reservations tab.
Why: skip duplicates â€” don't create the same reservation twice.

nodes.name: Split In Batches
nodes.notes:
Processes one booking at a time.
Why: prevents race conditions when writing to Sheets.

nodes.name: Update Stored Timestamp
nodes.notes:
Writes the new timestamp back to the sheet.
Why: next run will only pick up bookings after this point.

nodes.name: Compute Max After Loop
nodes.notes:
Finds the latest createdUtcDateTime from all processed bookings.
Why: this becomes the new 'last timestamp' for the next run.

nodes.name: Filter New Bookings
nodes.notes:
Keeps only BOOKED reservations created after the last timestamp.
Why: avoids re-processing old bookings.

nodes.name: Detect Extended Checkouts
nodes.notes:
Finds bookings where checkout time changed vs what's in the sheet.
Why: checkout changes need special handling (Workflow 1A).

nodes.name: Split Extended Candidates
nodes.notes:
Loops through each extended checkout one at a time.
Why: each one triggers a separate webhook call.

nodes.name: Lookup Reservation for Candidate
nodes.notes:
Gets the existing reservation data for the checkout-changed booking.
Why: we need the old checkout time to compare.

nodes.name: Reservation Exists Guard
nodes.notes:
Only proceeds if the reservation actually exists in the sheet.
Why: can't process an extended checkout for a booking we don't have.

nodes.name: Trigger Extended Checkout Handler
nodes.notes:
Sends a webhook to Workflow 1A with old/new checkout details.
Why: Workflow 1A handles all the updates (sheets, calendar, email).

nodes.name: Output Leads Individually
nodes.notes:
Splits the filtered list into individual items for the loop.
Why: Split In Batches needs single items.

nodes.name: Has More Pages?1
nodes.notes:
Checks if the API returned a next-page cursor.
Why: if yes, loop back and fetch more; if no, move on to filtering.

nodes.name: Accumulate Leads
nodes.notes:
Collects all reservation pages into a single list.
Why: we need all bookings together before filtering.

nodes.name: Fetch Hostfully Leads
nodes.notes:
Calls Hostfully API to get the first page of reservations.
Why: this is how we pull booking data into our system.

nodes.name: Initialize Cursor
nodes.notes:
Sets the pagination cursor to blank so we start from the first page.
Why: Hostfully API uses cursor-based pagination.

nodes.name: Read Last Timestamp
nodes.notes:
Reads the last-processed timestamp from the sheet.
Why: so we only process bookings newer than what we've already seen.

nodes.name: Schedule Trigger
nodes.notes:
Runs on a schedule to poll Hostfully for new bookings.
Why: we need to detect new reservations automatically.

nodes.name: Detect Cancellations
nodes.notes:
Finds bookings that changed to CANCELLED status in Hostfully.
Why: cancellations need cleanup (Cancellation Handler workflow).

nodes.name: Lookup Reservation for Cancellation
nodes.notes:
Gets the existing reservation for the cancelled booking.
Why: we need bookingUid and propertyUid to send to the handler.

nodes.name: Cancellation Idempotency Guard
nodes.notes:
Skips if the reservation is already marked CANCELLED in our sheet.
Why: prevents sending duplicate cancellation webhooks.

nodes.name: Trigger Cancellation Handler
nodes.notes:
Sends a webhook to the Cancellation Handler workflow.
Why: that workflow handles all cancellation cleanup.

parameters.content:
**Fetch Hostfully Leads1**

Fetches the next page of reservations using the cursor.
Why: some properties have more bookings than fit in one API page.

**Update Reservation with Cleaning Job ID**

Links the reservation to its cleaning job by writing the job reference back.
Why: keeps Reservations and CleaningJobs cross-referenced.

nodes.name: Note: Update Reservation with Cleaning Job ID

parameters.content:
**Create Cleaning Job Record**

Appends a new row to the CleaningJobs tab.
Why: creates the job that Phase 2 will pick up and assign to a cleaner.

**Prepare Cleaning Job Data**

Builds the cleaning job fields (date, time, status=PENDING, empty cleanerId).
Why: structures the data before writing to CleaningJobs.

**Create Reservation Record**

Appends a new row to the Reservations tab with booking details.
Why: this is our internal record of the booking.

**Cleaning Job Needed?**

Checks if a CleaningJobs row already exists for this booking.
Why: prevents duplicate cleaning job records.

**Reservation Exists?**

Branches: TRUE = already in sheet (skip), FALSE = new booking (create).
Why: prevents duplicate reservation records.

**Merge Lead and Lookup**

Combines the Hostfully data with any existing reservation data.
Why: gives us all fields in one object for the next decisions.

**Ensure One Item**

Guarantees exactly one item passes through, even if lookup returned nothing.
Why: downstream nodes expect one item per iteration.

**Lookup Reservation**

Checks if this bookingUid already exists in the Reservations tab.
Why: skip duplicates â€” don't create the same reservation twice.

**Split In Batches**

Processes one booking at a time.
Why: prevents race conditions when writing to Sheets.

**Update Stored Timestamp**

Writes the new timestamp back to the sheet.
Why: next run will only pick up bookings after this point.

**Compute Max After Loop**

Finds the latest createdUtcDateTime from all processed bookings.
Why: this becomes the new 'last timestamp' for the next run.

**Filter New Bookings**

Keeps only BOOKED reservations created after the last timestamp.
Why: avoids re-processing old bookings.

**Detect Extended Checkouts**

Finds bookings that:
1. Already exist in the sheet (created <= storedTimestamp)
2. Were recently updated in Hostfully (updated > storedTimestamp)

Attaches _hostfullyCheckoutUTC (UTC ISO) for comparison downstream.
newCleaningDate and newCleaningTime come from checkOutLocalDateTime
so they match local time (not UTC).

Note: this is a candidate list — actual checkout comparison
happens in Reservation Exists Guard.

**Split Extended Candidates**

Loops through each extended checkout one at a time.
Why: each one triggers a separate webhook call.

**Lookup Reservation for Candidate**

Gets the existing reservation row from the sheet for each candidate.
The stored checkOut field here is used by Reservation Exists Guard
to confirm the checkout actually changed (sheet vs Hostfully).

**Reservation Exists Guard**

Two-stage filter:
1. Reservation must exist in the sheet (has a stored checkOut)
2. Hostfully local checkout must be strictly LATER than stored local checkout

Comparison uses normalized local time strings (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SS)
on both sides — avoids the UTC parsing pitfall where a space-separated
sheet value ("2026-03-25 10:00:00") and a T-separated Hostfully value
("2026-03-25T10:00:00") would parse differently via new Date(), making
same-time checkouts look like extensions.

Strips internal _hostfullyCheckoutUTC field before passing downstream.

**Trigger Extended Checkout Handler**

Sends a webhook to Workflow 1A with old/new checkout details.
Why: Workflow 1A handles all the updates (sheets, calendar, email).

**Output Leads Individually**

Splits the filtered list into individual items for the loop.
Why: Split In Batches needs single items.

**Has More Pages?1**

Checks if the API returned a next-page cursor.
Why: if yes, loop back and fetch more; if no, move on to filtering.

**Accumulate Leads**

Collects all reservation pages into a single list.
Why: we need all bookings together before filtering.

**Fetch Hostfully Leads**

Calls Hostfully API to get the first page of reservations.
Why: this is how we pull booking data into our system.

**Initialize Cursor**

Sets the pagination cursor to blank so we start from the first page.
Why: Hostfully API uses cursor-based pagination.

**Read Last Timestamp**

Reads the last-processed timestamp from the sheet.
Why: so we only process bookings newer than what we've already seen.

**Schedule Trigger**

Runs on a schedule to poll Hostfully for new bookings.
Why: we need to detect new reservations automatically.

**Detect Cancellations**

Finds bookings that changed to CANCELLED status in Hostfully.
Why: cancellations need cleanup (Cancellation Handler workflow).

**Lookup Reservation for Cancellation**

Gets the existing reservation for the cancelled booking.
Why: we need bookingUid and propertyUid to send to the handler.

**Cancellation Idempotency Guard**

Skips if the reservation is already marked CANCELLED in our sheet.
Why: prevents sending duplicate cancellation webhooks.

**Trigger Cancellation Handler**

Sends a webhook to the Cancellation Handler workflow.
Why: that workflow handles all cancellation cleanup.

main.node: Update Reservation with Cleaning Job ID
